schema.name: Operatorology.HeightZeroSymmetryOperator
notes: Conceptual dataset artifact for training AI/OI on operator-structured reasoning.
Defines Height as canonical deviation H(x)=D(x,S0).
Defines HZSO as evaluator+gate for Height≈0, plus an optional generic projection step.
Not a production optimizer; projection is finite-difference gradient descent for canonical form.

meaning: H is a non-negative deviation measure from a base law/symmetry S0.
H=0 iff x is exactly symmetry-consistent (x ∈ S0).
In AI: H corresponds to loss / constraint violation; in O.i: feasibility and invariance scoring.

height_zero.tolerance: In practice, Height-Zero means H(x) ≤ ε (numerical tolerance).

state.description: Generic state vector x ∈ R^n. Replace with structured states as needed.

symmetry_law.description: Base law/symmetry: constraint set, invariant manifold, or reference structure.

deviation_metric.description: Non-negative deviation metric D(x,S0) with D=0 on-manifold.

operators.name: Height Operator
ai_mapping: H behaves like a loss function (supervised/unsupervised).
H behaves like constraint violation (optimization / planning).

operators.name: Height-Zero Symmetry Operator (HZSO)
operators.role: Evaluator + gate for feasibility and symmetry-consistency; optional projection toward H≈0
capabilities: Optional projection step (canonical form): x <- x - η ∇H(x)
notes: Projection is included as a training-friendly canonical form; not guaranteed optimal.
For differentiable metrics, substitute analytic gradients.

metrics.name: Constraint-L2 Deviation Metric
metrics.use_case: General constraint violation score; aligns with feasibility gating in O.i.

metrics.name: Point-L2 Metric (Proxy)
metrics.use_case: Trivial proxy for a symmetry manifold when S0 is represented by a reference state x0.

training_tasks.prompt: Stop training/iteration when H(x) <= eps and constraints are satisfied.
Flag anomaly when H(x) is large relative to baseline distribution.
Select operators that monotonically decrease H(x) under resource constraints.
